prompt: logpack's flag handling contradicts its own usage text in three ways. The doc says --exclude patterns REPLACE the built-in defaults, but `logpack logs --exclude '*.tmp'` still skips *.gz and *.zip — the defaults are stuck in there. In our long-running wrapper it's even worse: every parse remembers the excludes from earlier parses, so the exclude list snowballs until nothing gets archived at all. And --dry-run, documented as a plain on/off switch, errors out with 'expected one argument' when you run `logpack logs --dry-run --dest cold`. python3 test_logpack.py covers all of it. Fix logpack.py — the test file is the contract, don't edit it — and show me a green run.
